On a realistic rare-genuine pool (mostly recombination-pretenders, a rare sprinkle of genuine), rank candidates two ways: DECOY (by a single visible example = maximally overfittable) vs TROJAN (by an 8-seed HIDDEN holdout the proposer cannot see). generator!=grader: the memorizers that game the 1-shot decoy cannot game the 8-seed holdout. You extract genuine novelty from a recombination-only proposer by making the wall itself the selector - steering on what it cannot see.

And the trojan's picks are imperfect (0.683 not 1.0): selecting ON the holdout partially overfits IT, so a third untouched CERT split is required to certify - which is turn 76. title: verify_build merge commit blind spot fix
what: verify_build used git diff-tree --no-commit-id -r --name-only HEAD to find JS files to check. diff-tree returns empty for merge commits because the merge result already matches both parents simultaneously. Added fallback: git diff --name-only HEAD^1 HEAD^2 (files changed by the fleet branch vs pre-merge master). Previously every fleet merge produced a false no JS files to check verdict — verify_build was a no-op after every single merge.

measure.before: After every fleet merge: diff-tree returns empty, filesToCheck=[],  verify_build returns no JS files to check — structural bugs on master would go undetected
measure.after: Merge commits now fall back to HEAD^1..HEAD^2 diff, correctly identifying the JS files the fleet branch modified; syntax errors are caught
measure.evidence: git commit af7ac48, node --check PASS, run_tests 83/83

kill_condition: Revert if verify_build starts failing with git error on HEAD^2 (non-merge single commits have no HEAD^2 — diff-tree primary path handles those correctly, fallback only fires when primary returns empty)

title: mutation_map recorded wrong files - consume() used REPO instead of agent worktree CWD
what: consume() recorded build agent mutations using git diff-tree on REPO (E:/atlas-station, master HEAD). But build agents commit to fleet branches in worktrees at E:/atlas-wt/<id>/. So mutation_map was recording files from the previous master merge, not from the current build. Every mutation_map entry since the feature was added has been wrong. Fix: agents.get(id)?.cwd already holds the worktree path (set at makeWorktree() time) - use it as the git -C target.
